title: GTM Associate, London
key_evidence: Low hard bar: only 2+ yrs customer-facing experience in consulting/startups/sales/CS; Ryan's ~4.4 yrs operator/community/GTM clears this comfortably.
Explicit must-have: 'active AI power user leveraging tech for 10x productivity' — Ryan's Claude Code agent building is a direct, differentiating match.
UK work authorisation without sponsorship required — Ryan qualifies (UK-only auth).

Role spans demos, onboarding, conversion/retention, and 'own outcomes across GTM, operations, product, and hiring, launching manual experiments before scaling' — strongly matches Ryan's founding-generalist operator profile and first-employee experience.

Domain = AI for financial advisers / wealthtech (Blackbird Ventures, $12.7M raised in <1yr, 650+ firms, founded 2025). Outside Ryan's crypto/Web3 + AI-tooling domain; nice-to-have 'financial services exposure' is a gap, not a blocker.
Salary £40-70k + equity — band dips below the £50k floor at the low end but goes to £70k and is negotiable; in-person London with international travel.
Multiple parallel GTM/Growth roles open (also a Growth Lead, London) — moderate applicant pull; Blackbird brand raises visibility somewhat but lower hype than Jack & Jill.
biggest_lever: Frame the $4.5m presale + 50k->5k lifecycle conversion work as proven GTM conversion/retention outcomes, and lead the AI-power-user requirement with concrete Claude Code workflows — both are explicit must-haves Ryan can over-index on.
biggest_risk: Wealthtech/financial-advice domain is outside Ryan's crypto + AI-tooling expertise (nice-to-have FS exposure absent), and the role leans demo/sales-conversion which is adjacent to community-led growth rather than identical. Low salary floor risk (£40k bottom).
recency_note: Ashby publishedAt 2026-03-23 (~11 weeks / 78d old). Aging GTM req in the 8-12wk bracket (G=18); no updatedAt/repost signal, no applicant count visible. Recency pulls callback from 68 -> 55.
momentum_signals: FRESH FUNDING DIRECTLY FUNDING GTM (strong positive): $10M seed led by Blackbird Ventures, closed 2026-04-28 (Tech.eu, TechFundingNews, Pulse2). Total ~$12.7M raised in <1yr. Capital earmarked to 'expand UK and Australia presence and support entry into the US market' = GTM/expansion spend - exactly the function this role serves.
RAPID TRACTION: 650+ adviser firms onboarded across 6 countries in under a year with 'almost zero churn' (Blackbird portfolio page) - strong product-market-fit signal.

ACTIVELY HIRING GTM: confirmed 'actively hiring for GTM roles in 2026' across eng/GTM/design; parallel Growth Lead, London role open alongside this GTM Associate (Ashby marloo board) - GTM team being built out around co-founders Hardy + Shak.
HEADCOUNT TRAJECTORY: early-stage seed co scaling post-raise into 3 geographies = growing, not flat/shrinking.
NO RED FLAGS: no layoffs/down-round/pivot/founder-exit. Blackbird (first/largest Canva investor) backing adds credibility.
ROLE-CHURN: clean opening; aging 78-day Ashby date offset by post-raise expansion intent.
momentum_note: Green. Fresh $10M Blackbird seed (Apr 28 2026) explicitly funding UK/AU/US GTM expansion - the strongest possible momentum match for a GTM Associate seat. 650+ firms near-zero churn, parallel GTM roles open, no red flags. H=80 lifts callback despite stale 78-day posting. callback_v2 55 -> callback_v3 60.
